turnaround_reason: In-flight connectivity provider for business aviation down 72% from $16.82 high to $4.70 on competitive pressure (Starlink Aviation), integration drag from $375M Satcom Direct acquisition (Dec 2024), and weak 2025 results. New product portfolio launched: Gogo Galileo LEO service early 2025, Gogo 5G ATG network late 2025. Q1 2026: revenue $226.3M (-2%), Adj EBITDA $53.3M — operational stabilization in progress.
bullish_points: Two new networks rolling out in tandem (5G ATG + Galileo LEO) — multi-year refresh cycle should drive equipment + service revenue
Equipment revenue +22% YoY in Q1 2026 = early signal that aircraft are upgrading
Cross-sell opportunity into Satcom Direct customer base just beginning to materialize
Analyst target mean $9.50 (sell-side only 2 analysts but both Buy) = 102% upside; high target $12
21% short interest creates squeeze setup if Q2/Q3 shows revenue inflection
bearish_points: Starlink Aviation competitive threat is severe — Elon's network is genuinely better for many use cases at lower price
Debt-to-equity 765% — capital structure is extreme; $903M debt vs $107M cash limits flexibility
Revenue declining (-1.7% TTM) — the new networks haven't yet offset the legacy ATG decline
Q4 2025 EPS missed by 113% — execution risk during the integration is real
Insider activity is mixed; no clear conviction signal from management buying
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings August 6 — equipment revenue trajectory, Galileo subscriber adds, Satcom Direct cross-sell metrics, commentary on Starlink competitive impact
suggested_trade: Small spot position with tight stop. The valuation is right but Starlink risk is asymmetric — competitive moats in commodity connectivity erode fast. Consider it a 12-month thesis with 6-month confirmation needed (Q3 2026 earnings would be the moment of truth on Galileo traction).
key_risk: Starlink Aviation wins a major business jet OEM as default provider for new builds; legacy ATG attrition accelerates while Galileo adoption disappoints; debt refinancing in 2027 forces dilution.
would_skip_if: Q2 equipment revenue growth decelerates from +22%; Starlink announces major bizjet OEM win; Galileo subscriber count is not disclosed (signals weakness)
